# Scrum Poker Cards — Complete Documentation

> Free, real-time planning poker for agile teams with illustrated cards.

## What is Planning Poker?

Planning poker is an agile estimation technique where team members simultaneously reveal their effort estimates for a user story. This prevents anchoring bias — everyone commits to their estimate before seeing others' votes.

## How Scrum Poker Cards Works

1. **Create a session** at https://scrumpoker.work — enter your name, pick a card preset, click Create
2. **Share the room URL** with your team (e.g., https://scrumpoker.work/room/funny-unicorn-42)
3. **Everyone joins** by entering their name — no signup required
4. **Vote** by clicking a card from your hand at the bottom
5. **Host reveals** all votes simultaneously — see the results with stats
6. **Discuss & repeat** — start a new round for the next story

## Card Presets

### All Cards (default, 14 cards)
1 (Low hanging fruit), 2 (Piece of cake), 3 (It ain't rocket science), 5 (Weird but doable), 8 (An arm and a leg), 13 (Squeaking by), 20 (Don't put all your eggs in one basket), 40 (In a real pickle), 100 (Monster task), Infinity (When pigs fly), ? (Here be dragons), Coffee (Coffee break), Brownie (Eat a brownie), Yak (Yak Shaving)

### Fibonacci
1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 13, 21, ?, Coffee

### Simple
1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 13, ?, Coffee

### T-Shirt Sizes
XS, S, M, L, XL, ?, Coffee

### Powers of 2
1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 32, ?, Coffee

## Features

### Confidence Score
When voting, players indicate their confidence: Confident, Guessing, or No Idea. Results show aggregate team confidence (HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW).

### Topic Queue
The host can pre-add a list of stories to vote through in order. The team advances through them with auto-progression. At the end, a summary table shows all estimates.

### Outlier Detection
After reveal, if someone's vote is far from the group median, the app prompts them to explain. Explanations are shared with the team.

### Fun Reveal Messages
Based on vote patterns: "Ship it!" (consensus), "We have no idea" (huge spread), "This sprint is doomed" (all high), etc.

### Host Controls
- Reveal votes / Start new round
- Set topic per round
- Configure card preset
- Kick players (with confirmation)
- Transfer host role to another player

### Session Properties
- Ephemeral: sessions exist only in memory
- No database, no persistence
- No accounts or authentication
- Real-time via WebSocket (PartyKit on Cloudflare Workers)
- Supports any number of players

## Card Illustrations

The card illustrations were created by Redbooth for their internal sprint planning. Each card features a playful idiom matching the effort level. They are licensed under Creative Commons Attribution (CC BY 3.0).

Original repository: https://github.com/redbooth/scrum-poker-cards

## Privacy

- No accounts or personal data collected
- Anonymous analytics via PostHog (memory persistence, no cookies)
- Error tracking via Sentry (technical data only)
- Player names stored in browser localStorage only
- Sessions are ephemeral — nothing saved server-side

## Technical Details

- Frontend: Vite + React 19 + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS v4 + Framer Motion
- Real-time: PartyKit (WebSocket rooms on Cloudflare Workers)
- Hosting: Cloudflare Pages
- Player identity: UUID in localStorage (stable across reconnects)
- Tab coordination: BroadcastChannel API

## Author

Built by Luiz Tanure (https://github.com/letanure)
Source: https://github.com/letanure/Scrum-poker-cards
Website: https://scrumpoker.work
